title: Maximum Subarray Sum

content.problemStatement:
Given an integer array numsnumsnums, find the **subarray** with the largest sum, and print its sum.

**Note**: A subarray is a contiguous non-empty sequence of elements within an array.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT, the number of test cases.
The first line in each test case contains NNN, the number of elements in an array.
The second line in each test case contains NNN integers, denoting the elements in the array.


Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum subarray sum of each array.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum possible sum of any non‑empty contiguous subarray.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First array: maximum subarray is [4, -1, 2, 1] with sum 6. Second array: only element 1. Third array: whole array sum 23.
All numbers are negative, so the largest (least negative) single element -1 is the answer.
All numbers are positive, therefore the whole array gives the maximum sum 15.

solution.approach: The problem asks for the maximum sum over all contiguous subarrays. The optimal solution is Kadane's algorithm, which scans the array once while maintaining the best subarray ending at the current position (cur) and the overall best seen so far (best). For each element x we update cur = max(x, cur + x); best = max(best, cur). This works because any optimal subarray either starts at the current element or extends the previous optimal ending subarray. The algorithm runs in linear time and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
